description_long: This is a metallic locking pliers tool, commonly known as vise-grips, featuring a robust, solid steel construction. The handles are straight and slightly tapered, ending in a rounded adjustment screw at the base for tension control. The jaws are broad and flat with a serrated gripping surface, designed to clamp securely onto objects of varying shapes and sizes. A visible pivot mechanism connects the two handles, allowing the jaws to open and close with a locking action. The surface is matte gray, showing signs of wear and utilitarian use, with no visible rubber or plastic insulation. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the tool.
description: A sturdy, metallic locking pliers tool with straight handles, serrated jaws, and a matte gray finish. It features a prominent adjustment screw and a robust pivot mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the straight handles, adjustment screw, and the broad, serrated jaws.
description_view_1: Front-angled view emphasizing the open jaws and the alignment of the handles, with the adjustment screw visible at the rear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the pliers, with the adjustment screw and jaw mechanism clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear-angled view focusing on the handles and adjustment screw, with the jaws partially visible in the background.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, metallic, non-insulated handles with a rounded adjustment screw at the end.
attribute_jaw_design: Broad, flat, serrated jaws designed for locking and gripping, typical of vise-grip pliers.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed, compound locking pivot with an adjustable screw for jaw tension and a release lever.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metallic surface with no visible rubber or textured grip pattern.
